title: Post-gastroenteritis follow-up

red_flags.text: Lists shock, severe dehydration, intractable vomiting, severe abdominal pain, prolonged symptoms, older age, comorbidity, pregnancy, antibiotic exposure, nocturnal diarrhea, and bloody or black stool.
red_flags.rationale: The exact red-flag section supports clinician-assessed hydration, general status, and concern documentation without asserting absence.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists shock, severe dehydration, intractable vomiting, severe abdominal pain, prolonged symptoms, older age, comorbidity, pregnancy, antibiotic exposure, nocturnal diarrhea, and bloody or black stool.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Gastroenteritis in Adults – 41

red_flags.text: Defines emergency and face-to-face referral features for adult gastroenteritis.
red_flags.rationale: The exact section supports clinician-entered safety net, escalation status, and follow-up without assigning urgency.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Defines emergency and face-to-face referral features for adult gastroenteritis.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Gastroenteritis in Adults – 41

investigations.text: Describes history-based assessment and investigation for suspected nonviral or complicated gastroenteritis.
investigations.rationale: The exact section supports recording existing laboratory or stool results without ordering tests.
investigations.evidence_extract: Describes history-based assessment and investigation for suspected nonviral or complicated gastroenteritis.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Gastroenteritis in Adults – 41

medication.text: Defines acute diarrheal illness and records stool frequency, duration, nausea, vomiting, fever, abdominal pain, exposure pattern, and dehydration features.
medication.rationale: The exact DHA adult section supports interval diarrhoea, vomiting, abdominal symptoms, intake, hydration history, fever, blood, travel, exposure, medicine, and duration without confirming ongoing infection.
medication.evidence_extract: Defines acute diarrheal illness and records stool frequency, duration, nausea, vomiting, fever, abdominal pain, exposure pattern, and dehydration features.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Gastroenteritis in Adults – 41

source_limitations: Original diagnosis, organism, current stool pattern, hydration, fever, bleeding, tests, diagnosis, treatment, return to work, referral, and interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
